strengths: 13+ years of full-stack engineering across web, mobile, desktop and embedded — directly matches the 6+ year requirement with significant headroom
Current role at re:cinq explicitly covers TypeScript/Node.js product work plus AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, hitting the JD's AI agent development requirement
Polyglot proficiency spanning TypeScript, Java, C/C++, DLang, PHP, Swift — aligns with the JD's call for multiple programming languages and Kotlin/Java secondary stack readiness
concerns: Resume lists no explicit Nest.js, Vitest, or Kotlin experience — primary stack alignment must be inferred from adjacent TypeScript/Node/Jest-style work
Cloud exposure is partial: AWS CDK is mentioned at Optilyz and Kubernetes at GISCollective, but GCP/Azure breadth is not demonstrated
React is referenced only briefly (Optilyz pricing team) relative to the heavier frontend expectations of a fullstack role

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience
Software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, code reviews, testing workshops)

response.justification: Bogdan brings 13+ years of full-stack delivery with a current Staff Engineer role at re:cinq that directly covers the JD's headline asks — TypeScript/Node.js product work plus hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generation, an unusually strong match for the AI agent requirement. His track record at Optilyz (TypeScript, AWS CDK, React, MongoDB at million-mail scale), RIDE Capital (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development with Node/TypeScript/GraphQL), and Dunnhumby (low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests/second) demonstrates the architecture, scalability, and performance instincts the role needs. Polyglot fluency across TypeScript, Java, C/C++, and DLang, combined with documented teaching, workshops, and open-source platform ownership (GISCollective on Kubernetes), signals exactly the customer-oriented craftsman the team is hiring for.
